title: Exorcise Driver code from libpod/define
body: The libpod/define code should not import any large dependencies, as it is intended to be structures and definitions only. It included the libpod/driver package for information on the storage driver, though, which brought in all of c/storage. Split the driver package so that define has the struct, and thus does not need to import Driver. And simplify the driver code while we're at it.

comments.body:

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

If we were develop a `libpod/utils` package, `driver` is a prime candidate for consolidating into that package. I don't really want to put it in `pkg/util` because I feel like that might be used by `cmd/podman` or the bindings...

We really need to come up with consistent location and naming for CLI-safe and non-CLI-safe util functions.
